Title: Azolla ferns testify: seed plants and ferns share a common ancestor for leucoanthocyanidin reductase enzymes

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to ecological fitness; PA in Azolla may act as antifeedants and may affect symbiont interaction and biomass digestibility.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fern-specific paralogous LAR-like clades WLAR1 and WLAR2 were identified by phylogenomics. 2. WLAR proteins differ from bona fide LAR in key substrate-binding residues while retaining NADPH-binding features. 3. Their biochemical role in PA metabolism remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic analysis; sequence alignment of substrate-binding residues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Found in A. filiculoides and other ferns; specific expression pattern not established.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, ENA PRJEB39515; phylogenomic analysis using 1KP orthogroup database v.2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because enzymatic function is not validated; proposed as a novel fern-specific LAR-like reductase of unknown function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential future target to dissect fern-specific PA polymerization or branch chemistry once biochemical function is resolved.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to ecological fitness; PA in Azolla may act as antifeedants and may affect symbiont interaction and biomass digestibility.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WLAR2 is a fern-specific LAR-like paralogous clade identified together with WLAR1. 2. Proteins in this clade have altered substrate-binding residues relative to LAR and PCBER. 3. Their role in Azolla PA metabolism is hypothesized but not demonstrated.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic analysis; sequence alignment of substrate-binding residues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Found in A. filiculoides and other ferns; specific expression pattern not established.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, ENA PRJEB39515; phylogenomic analysis using 1KP orthogroup database v.2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because its metabolic role is unknown and no direct enzyme assay or in vivo metabolite causality was shown.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential research target for engineering fern PA properties after biochemical characterization.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point candidate but not definitively proven rate-limiting; highly expressed and proposed as a key evolutionary innovation for PA biosynthesis in ferns.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to catechin/trans-flavan-3-ols using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and supplies flavan-3-ol units for proanthocyanidin formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely antifeedant through protein aggregation; may also influence symbiont interaction and biomass digestibility.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: sporophyte biomass; vacuoles of specialized trichomes in leaf cavities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes catechin formation in vitro and is strongly associated with high proanthocyanidin accumulation in Azolla sporophytes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A. filiculoides accumulates high levels of procyanidin-type PA and AzfiLAR transcript is among the most abundant secondary-metabolism enzyme transcripts. 2. Recombinant AzfiLAR produced catechin in vitro when supplied with leucoanthocyanidin generated by VvDFR from dihydroquercetin. 3. Phylogeny places AzfiLAR at the base of characterized seed-plant LARs, indicating LAR evolved in the common ancestor of ferns and seed plants. 4. High AzfiLAR expression is proposed to contribute to PA abundance and possibly PA degree of polymerization control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; recombinant protein expression and purification; in vitro enzyme assay coupled with VvDFR; LC-MS product identification; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very highly expressed in sporophytes; transcript peaks 2 h into the light period under nitrogen-free conditions and is reduced/altered by nitrogen supply; among the highest accumulating secondary-metabolism transcripts.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very highly expressed in A. filiculoides sporophytes during diel cycle, co-regulated with CHS-like, CHI-like and DFR-like genes.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VvDFR (used in coupled in vitro assay)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Diel cycle; nitrogen supply (2 mM NH4NO3) affected transcript peak pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of A. filiculoides diel and nitrogen treatments; ENA PRJEB39515; phylogenomics using 1KP orthogroup database v.2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High PA accumulation can reduce biomass digestibility and protein extraction efficiency; may limit inclusion of Azolla in animal diets.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to engineer PA level and possibly polymer properties in ferns for altered digestibility, ecological fitness, and symbiosis studies; in vivo editing tools for Azolla are not yet established.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors discuss that seed-plant LAR can also cleave Cys-EP to epicatechin; AzfiLAR may have similar dual catalysis, but this was not tested. No highly expressed ANR candidate was found, strengthening the central role proposed for AzfiLAR.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols such as dihydroquercetin to leucoanthocyanidins using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provided leucoanthocyanidin substrate in the coupled in vitro assay that enabled demonstration of AzfiLAR catechin-forming activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvDFR was used in a coupled assay because leucoanthocyanidins are unstable and unavailable commercially. 2. Catechin was produced only when VvDFR and AzfiLAR were both present with dihydroquercetin and NADPH. 3. This supported assignment of AzfiLAR as an active LAR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coupled enzyme assay; LC-MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay conditions at pH 6.5 with NADPH and DHQ
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzymatic partner for reconstructing fern LAR reactions in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is not the main subject gene of the paper but is experimentally central in the results as the direct precursor-generating enzyme in the validation assay.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: R2R3-MYB transcription factor, class VIII-D (NOECK/MYB106 clade-like)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: putative control of flavonoid/proanthocyanidin biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS-like; CHI-like; DFR-like; LAR-like genes (putative, based on correlated expression only)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes indirectly to PA-associated ecological fitness and antifeedant properties.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate regulator correlated with PA-biosynthetic gene expression, but no direct functional evidence provided.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established; highlighted as a highly expressed candidate regulator rather than a proven master regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Class VIII-D R2R3-MYBs were more highly expressed than class VIII-E in A. filiculoides. 2. Azfi_s0001.g000839 belongs to the highly expressed VIII-D clade containing AtNOECK/MYB106-like factors. 3. Correlated transcript changes with PA-pathway genes suggest possible involvement in FBP regulation, but direct targets were not tested.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during diel cycle relative to class VIII-E MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in A. filiculoides sporophytes during diel sampling.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Diel/light regime; nitrogen availability likely influences coordinated pathway expression.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Authors discuss possible MYB-bHLH-WD40-like regulatory complex by analogy, but no direct evidence for this gene.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential future regulatory handle to alter PA accumulation in Azolla once direct function is validated.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory candidate because it is specifically highlighted in Results as one of the most expressed potential R2R3-MYB regulators associated with PA-biosynthesis expression.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: R2R3-MYB transcription factor, class VIII-D (NOECK/MYB106 clade-like)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: putative control of flavonoid/proanthocyanidin biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS-like; CHI-like; DFR-like; LAR-like genes (putative, based on correlated expression only)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes indirectly to PA-associated ecological fitness and antifeedant properties.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate regulator correlated with PA-biosynthetic gene expression, but no direct functional evidence provided.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established; highlighted as a highly expressed candidate regulator rather than a proven master regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Azfi_s0016.g014344 is one of the most highly expressed class VIII-D R2R3-MYBs in A. filiculoides. 2. Its expression pattern supports the hypothesis that fern PA pathway genes are coordinately regulated. 3. Direct metabolic regulatory causality remains untested.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during diel cycle relative to class VIII-E MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in A. filiculoides sporophytes during diel sampling.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Diel/light regime; nitrogen availability likely influences coordinated pathway expression.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible MYB-bHLH-WD40-like regulatory context discussed by analogy only.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential future target for modulating PA levels and fern-symbiont/ecological traits after functional validation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence remains correlative; not demonstrated by transactivation or mutant analysis.
